LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT ANTHONY JOSEPH'S DEMURRER TO ALL FOUR CLAIMS ALLEGED IN DEFENDANT ADVERCAR'S 1ST AMENDED AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. ALL FOUR CLAIMS ARE ADEQUATELY PLED AND HAVE CURED THE DEFICIENCIES IDENTIFIED IN THE JANUARY 19, 2016 ORDER SUSTAINING MR. JOSEPH'S DEMURRER TO THE CROSS-COMPLAINT. THE AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES: 1) INDEPENDENTLY WRONGFUL CONDUCT BY MR. JOSEPH THAT INTERFERED WITH ADVERCAR'S ALLEGED PROSPECTIVE ECONOMIC ADVANTAGES; 2) MR. JOSEPH'S KNOWLEDGE THAT HIS CONDUCT WOULD INTERFERE WITH THOSE ADVANTAGES; 3) MR. JOSEPH'S BREACH OF A DUTY GIVING RISE TO A NEGLIGENCE-BASED CLAIM; AND 4) THE EXISTENCE OF A WRITTEN CONTRACT SUPPORTING A CLAIM FOR BREACH OF AN IMPLIED COVENANT THEREIN. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-17-2016 Set For Trial On Sep-05-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Declaration Of Jeanentte A. Vaccaro
Off Calendar May-16-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF ANTHONY JOSEPH'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint is granted. The motion is favored by the strong California policies of allowing amendments to pleadings and deciding cases on their merits. Nor will defendant be prejudiced by the granting of the motion. Defendant's argument that the changes to the complaint are substantively deficient is better addressed on a motion challenging the sufficiency of a pleading such as a demurrer or a motion to strike rather than on a motion to amend. Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-14-2016 Set For Trial On Mar-20-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Apr-27-2016 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-07-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jeanette Vaccaro of McGuinn, Hillsman & Palefsky telephone number 415-421-9292 for Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant Anthony Joseph.
Brian Maschler of Gordon & Rees, LLP telephone number 415-986-5900 for Defendant/Cross-complainant AdverCar, Inc.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT ANTHONY JOSEPH'S DEMURRER to all four claims alleged in defendant AdverCar's 1st amended amended cross-complaint is overruled. All four claims are adequately pled and have cured the deficiencies identified in the January 19, 2016 order sustaining Mr. Joseph's demurrer to the cross-complaint. The amended cross-complaint sufficiently alleges: 1) independently wrongful conduct by Mr. Joseph that interfered with adverCar's alleged prospective economic advantages; 2) Mr. Joseph's knowledge that his conduct would interfere with those advantages; 3) Mr. Joseph's breach of a duty giving rise to a negligence-based claim; and 4) the existence of a written contract supporting a claim for breach of an implied covenant therein. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-09-2016 Continued To Apr-27-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-19-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT ANTHONY JOSEPH'S DEMURRER TO DEFENDANT'S CROSS-COMPLAINT - 1) Demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to Defendant's claim of intentional interference with prospective economic relations. Defendant does not state facts sufficient to show that Plaintiff knew that his alleged interference was certain or substantially certain to occur as a result of its action. (Korea Supply Co. v. Lockheed Martin Corp. (2003) 29 Cal.4th 1134, 1153.) Defendants also does not state facts sufficient to show that Plaintiff's acts were blameworthy outside of the alleged interference itself. Accordingly, demurrer is sustained as to the first cause of action. (Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) (2) Demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to Defendant's claim of negligent interference with prospective economic relations. Defendant does not show that Plaintiff breached a duty of care for negligent interference as set forth in J'Aire Corp. v. Gregory (1979) 24 Cal.3d 799, 804. (3) Demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to Defendant's claim of breach of covenant of good faith and fair dealing. When a claim is founded upon a contract, a demurrer is proper where "it cannot be ascertained from the pleading whether the contract is written, is oral, or is implied by conduct. (Code Civ. Pro. 430.10(g).) Here, Defendants do not plainly allege a contract. Plaintiff and Defendants had a revised commission arrangement and independent contractor relationship, but it is unknown whether this arrangement is based on written, oral, or implied conduct. (4) Demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to Defendant's claim of unfair business practices. Defendant contends that Plaintiff made misrepresentations to a potential investor and to Defendant's largest customer, tried to terminate Defendant's CEO and CFO, and influenced Defendant to discontinue business operations. This cause of action is intentionally broad to accommodate " 'new schemes which the fertility of man's invention would contrive.' " [Citation.]' [Citation.]" (Community Assisting Recovery, Inc. v. Aegis Security Ins. Co. (2001) 92 Cal.App.4th 886, 894.) However, demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to this cause of action because it is based on Defendant's other causes of action. Order signed.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)
